figures.caption: Figure 1: The difference of the perception of recommendations between real users and LLM-based user simulators.

Figure 7: Supplementary qualitative comparison of slot-level attention. Each row is one session (user × task); columns are frozen Qwen3-VL (Backbone), human fixation, and FixATE after personalized tuning. Posters are blurred where required for copyright.
Table 3: Evaluation of AttnLRP on the AdSERP dataset.

Table 4: Extended evaluation on RecGaze with standard deviations and significance levels (cf. Table 1 in the main paper). ∗ 𝑝 < 0.05, ∗∗ 𝑝 < 0.01 (two-sided paired 𝑡-test, df = 4). Values are reported as mean± std. Backbone values are deterministic (frozen model, greedy decoding) and thus reported without variance. Bold indicates improvement over Backbone.
Figure 3: Overview of the Fixation-aligned Tuning for User Emulation (FixATE) framework.
